channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: ‘It’s a failed nation’: Trump pressures Cuba as fuel crisis deepens
item.description: US President Donald Trump has called Cuba ‘a failed nation’, as his administration expands its pressure campaign.

item.title: Argentines protest against Milei’s defunding of public universities
item.description: Argentines protest against Milei’s defunding of public universities.

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel fires back at drinking allegations
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel and Senator Chris Van Hollen had a heated exchange during a Senate budget hearing.

item.title: Peru presidential candidate Roberto Sanchez charged with financial crimes
item.description: Prosecutor calls for leftist candidate to be jailed for five years and four months over false financial disclosures.

item.title: Starmer at risk because he pushed Labour to be ‘new Conservative Party’
item.description: Author Oliver Eagleton explains why British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is fighting for his job.

item.title: Iran war live: Trump travels to China as conflict with Tehran looms large
item.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed 2,883 people and injured 8,787 since March 2, Lebanon's Health Ministry says.

item.title: Chinese firm unveils ‘transformer’ style manned robot
item.description: Unitree Robotics has released footage of its CEO piloting the GD01 transformable mecha that smashes through walls.

item.title: Honduras mayor arrested for masterminding environmentalist’s killing
item.description: Adan Funez was one of three men arrested this week over alleged involvement in the shooting of activist Juan Lopez.

item.title: Sam Altman says Elon Musk wanted 90 percent of OpenAI in high-stakes trial
item.description: Landmark trial could shape future and leadership of OpenAI as company prepares for possible initial public offering.

item.title: Basketball star Brandon Clarke, Memphis Grizzlies forward, dies at age 29
item.description: Neither the Grizzlies nor Clarke’s agency, Priority Sports, immediately released details surrounding his death.

item.title: Trump says he doesn’t need Xi’s help on Iran war as he heads to China
item.description: Trump gives conflicting messages on prominence of Iran war in upcoming talks with China's Xi, emphasising trade.

item.title: US court pauses decision blocking Trump’s 10 percent global tariff
item.description: A coalition of 24 states have argued that Trump's latest tariffs do not meet the standards of 1974 Trade Act.

item.title: Cannes juror denounces Hollywood boycott of actors for Gaza war views
item.description: 2026 Cannes Film Festival juror Paul Laverty denounced Hollywood for boycotting actors who’ve opposed the Gaza war.

item.title: Trump backs Pakistan as Iran mediator after criticism from Lindsey Graham
item.description: US president lauds Islamabad, but his Republican ally says he does not trust Pakistan to facilitate Iran diplomacy.

item.title: Why have peace efforts failed to end conflict in Sudan?
item.description: The UN says the use of drones is making the conflict more dangerous.

item.title: Red Cross says people displaced by conflict in Colombia doubled last year
item.description: Annual report by ICRC finds worsening conditions for Colombian civilians amid continued fighting between armed groups.

item.title: Why is Iran increasingly targeting the UAE in its war messaging?
item.description: The UAE's close ties to the US and Israel have placed the Gulf country under Iranian suspicion.

item.title: Ukraine hits Russia’s distant gas facilities after Moscow’s attacks kill 6
item.description: The attack comes in retaliation for the Russian attacks that killed at least six in Ukraine, according to the president.

item.title: Moment taxi driver wrestles armed man during police chase in Turkiye
item.description: A taxi driver wrestled a gunman that was forcing him to drive during a police chase in Turkiye.

item.title: Macron unveils $27 billion Africa investment, urges reset with Europe
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron, in Kenya for the Africa Forward summit, called for a ‘fundamental reset’ in Africa.

item.title: Trump skirts question on US ‘red lines’ for Iran ceasefire
item.description: Trump skirted a question from a reporter about whether he has a ‘red line’ that would end the ceasefire with Iran.

item.title: ‘Cotton picking’: US lawmaker condemned for racist comment about Jeffries
item.description: US representative Jen Kiggans appears to endorse racist statements about top House Democrat Hakeem Jeffries.

item.title: Kevin Warsh confirmed to US Federal Reserve board in close Senate vote
item.description: Senator John Fetterman of Pennsylvania, a Democrat, joins Republicans to advance Warsh's confirmation.

item.title: Serbia hosts first joint military exercise with NATO
item.description: The two-week-long drills come less than 30 years after NATO bombed Belgrade in the Kosovo war.

item.title: Voters head to the polls in the Bahamas for high-stakes snap election
item.description: Affordability, wage growth, and housing costs dominate Bahamian snap election as parties vie for 41 parliamentary seats.
